data.name: Bella Napoli
data.description: Authentic Neapolitan pizza baked in a wood-fired oven imported straight from Naples. The margherita is a must, but the truffle burrata will steal the show.

data.name: Tokyo Ramen House
data.description: Rich tonkotsu broth simmered for 18 hours, topped with chashu pork and a perfectly soft-boiled egg. Cash only, but absolutely worth the queue.

data.name: Le Petit Bistro
data.description: Classic French bistro with an ever-changing seasonal menu. The duck confit and crème brûlée are permanent fixtures for good reason. Reservations strongly recommended.

data.name: Taco Fiesta
data.description: Family-run taqueria serving slow-cooked birria and al pastor tacos since 1998. The salsa verde is made fresh every morning and the horchata is cold and creamy.

data.name: The Curry Palace
data.description: Fragrant curries and freshly baked naan from a tandoor oven. The butter chicken is a crowd favourite, while the lamb rogan josh is deeply spiced and comforting.

data.name: Dragon Garden
data.description: Dim sum by day, Cantonese roasts by night. The char siu pork is lacquered to perfection and the XO fried rice is generously loaded with seafood.

data.name: Smoky BBQ Joint
data.description: Low-and-slow smoked brisket, pulled pork, and baby back ribs. Everything is smoked on-site over hickory wood and served with house-made pickles and cornbread.

data.name: Golden Chopsticks
data.description: Steaming bowls of pho broth simmered overnight with star anise and charred ginger. The bánh mì is loaded with house-pickled daikon and crispy pork belly.

data.name: El Asador
data.description: Wood-fire parrilla steakhouse serving perfectly charred cuts of ribeye and skirt steak. The chimichurri is made tableside and the Malbec list is exceptional.

data.name: Sakura Sushi
data.description: Omakase-style counter where the chef sources fish daily from Tsukiji. The otoro nigiri melts on the tongue and the house ponzu is a closely guarded secret.

data.name: Mama Ethiopia
data.description: Generous sharing platters of injera topped with slow-cooked doro wat and misir. Everything is made from scratch and the spiced honey wine is not to be missed.

data.name: La Maison du Crêpe
data.description: Buckwheat galettes and sweet crêpes flambéed at the table. The salted caramel and Grand Marnier crêpe Suzette are crowd favourites on a cosy Breton-themed terrace.

data.name: Spice Route
data.description: Punchy green curries and fragrant pad kra pao cooked over high flame. The som tam papaya salad is genuinely fiery and the mango sticky rice is a perfect finish.

data.name: Trattoria da Marco
data.description: Family trattoria serving hand-rolled pasta and slow-braised ragù that has been on the menu since 1972. The tiramisu is made fresh each morning and is never too sweet.

data.name: The Greek Corner
data.description: Charcoal-grilled souvlaki, creamy tzatziki and flaky spanakopita baked in-house. The barrel retsina and sea-view terrace make this a reliable warm-weather spot.

data.name: Umami Burger Lab
data.description: Smash burgers with a double-sear crust, aged cheddar and a proprietary umami sauce. The truffle fries are dangerously addictive and the shakes are served frozen-thick.

data.name: Pearl of the Sea
data.description: Whole lobster, oysters flown in daily and a bouillabaisse that takes two days to prepare. Floor-to-ceiling windows face the water for an unmatched dining atmosphere.

data.name: Seoul Kitchen
data.description: Table-top BBQ with marinated galbi and bulgogi, plus a rotating lineup of twelve banchan. The kimchi jjigae is deeply fermented and the cold naengmyeon is a revelation.

data.name: Pâtisserie Céleste
data.description: Award-winning pâtisserie known for laminated croissants with 81 layers and seasonal tarts that sell out before 9am. The pistachio éclair has a cult following.

data.name: Casa Oaxaca
data.description: Regional Mexican cooking rooted in Oaxacan tradition. The seven-chili mole negro is the star, slow-cooked for eight hours and served over turkey with fresh tortillas.

data.name: The Dim Sum Club
data.description: Weekend dim sum institution with push carts, crystal prawn har gow and flaky egg tarts fresh from the oven. Arrive early — the queues form before opening.

data.name: Monsoon Table
data.description: Contemporary Indian tasting menu reinterpreting street food classics. The chaat trolley, smoked dal makhani and cardamom kulfi are the headline acts.

data.name: La Taquiza
data.description: No-frills street taco stand that went brick-and-mortar. Carnitas, lengua, and crispy chicharrón tacos on freshly pressed corn tortillas with three housemade salsas.

data.name: Brasserie Nord
data.description: Classic Belgian brasserie with moules-frites cooked six ways and a beer list of over forty Trappist and lambic ales. The waterzooi is a warming weekend staple.

data.name: Levant & Mezze
data.description: Abundant mezze spread of hand-rolled falafel, silky hummus and fresh fattoush. The mixed grill platter and baklava tower are the best way to end a long table feast.

data.name: Noodle Republic
data.description: Hawker-style kopitiam serving penang char kway teow with wok hei, laksa with coconut-prawn broth and crispy roti canai dipped in dhal. Queue-worthy and worth every minute.

data.name: Osteria del Porto
data.description: Seafood-focused osteria where the catch changes daily. The spaghetti alle vongole is briny and perfect, the grilled branzino arrives whole, and the Sicilian cannoli seals the deal.
